title: Takeaways From The Iran Peace Deal
text:
Takeaways From The Iran Peace Deal

Louis-Vincent Gave

Gavekal Research | 15 Jun 2026

A number of “Iran peace deals” have been proclaimed over the past two months. However, this latest one does feel more genuine than its predecessors. First, it is being confirmed by a third party, Pakistan. Second, the Iranian leadership has announced that a memorandum of understanding has been agreed. Third, an official signing date, Friday, has been announced. Of course, between now and Friday, the deal could be derailed, either by hard-line Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps members renewing attacks on Gulf states and/or Israel (as Mao Zedong famously said, power lies at the end of the gun), or by renewed military action by Israel in Lebanon, Gaza or Iran itself. Still, a peace deal is now a high-probability outcome, even if not yet a certainty. Assuming that by next weekend, the Strait of Hormuz is once again open to maritime traffic, and that the Gulf states no longer have to worry about drone or missile strikes, what should be our takeaways?
